2604.05172.title: ClawsBench: Evaluating Capability and Safety of LLM Productivity Agents in Simulated Workspaces
2604.05172.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed to automate productivity tasks (e.g., email, scheduling, document management), but evaluating them on live services is risky due to potentially irreversible changes. Existing benchmarks rely on simplified environments and fail to capture realistic, stateful, multi-service workflows. We introduce ClawsBench, a benchmark for evaluating and improving LLM agents in realistic productivity settings. It includes five high-fidelity mock services (Gmail, Slack, Google Calendar, Google Docs, Google Drive) with full state management and deterministic snapshot/restore, along with 44 structured tasks covering single-service, cross-service, and safety-critical scenarios. We decompose agent scaffolding into two independent levers (domain skills that inject API knowledge via progressive disclosure, and a meta prompt that coordinates behavior across services) and vary both to measure their separate and combined effects. Experiments across 6 models, 4 agent harnesses, and 33 conditions show that with full scaffolding, agents achieve task success rates of 39-64% but exhibit unsafe action rates of 7-33%. On OpenClaw, the top five models fall within a 10 percentage-point band on task success (53-63%), with unsafe action rates from 7% to 23% and no consistent ordering between the two metrics. We identify eight recurring patterns of unsafe behavior, including multi-step sandbox escalation and silent contract modification.
2604.05172.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）代理人越來越多地被部署來自動化生產力任務（例如，電子郵件、排程、文件管理），但在實時服務上評估它們是有風險的，因為可能會造成不可逆的變化。現有的基準依賴於簡化的環境，未能捕捉現實的、有狀態的、多服務的工作流程。我們介紹了 ClawsBench，一個用於在現實生產力環境中評估和改善 LLM 代理人的基準。它包括五個高保真模擬服務（Gmail、Slack、Google 日曆、Google 文件、Google 雲端硬碟），具備完整的狀態管理和確定性快照/恢復，並涵蓋 44 個結構化任務，涉及單一服務、跨服務和安全關鍵場景。我們將代理人支架分解為兩個獨立的槓桿（通過逐步披露注入 API 知識的領域技能，以及協調跨服務行為的元提示），並變化這兩者以測量它們的單獨和聯合效果。在 6 個模型、4 個代理人框架和 33 種條件下的實驗顯示，當具備完整支架時，代理人的任務成功率為 39-64%，但不安全行為的比例為 7-33%。在 OpenClaw 上，前五名模型的任務成功率在 10 個百分點的範圍內（53-63%），不安全行為的比例從 7% 到 23% 不等，且兩個指標之間沒有一致的排序。我們識別出八種重複出現的不安全行為模式，包括多步沙盒升級和靜默合約修改。
2604.05172.author: Xiangyi Li et.al.
2604.05172.authors: Xiangyi Li, Kyoung Whan Choe, Yimin Liu, Xiaokun Chen, Chujun Tao, Bingran You, Wenbo Chen, Zonglin Di, Jiankai Sun, Shenghan Zheng, Jiajun Bao, Yuanli Wang, Weixiang Yan, Yiyuan Li, Han-chung Lee
